plain_definition: Switzerland's integrated financial regulator, supervising banks, securities firms, insurers, collective investment schemes, and financial market infrastructure such as stock exchanges and central securities depositories. FINMA has no dedicated digital-asset licence category; instead it applies Switzerland's existing, technology-neutral financial-market laws to crypto-asset business under a 'same risks, same rules' approach, publishing supervisory guidance (such as its 2018 ICO guidelines and 2024 stablecoin guidance) rather than new statutes, and licensing digital-asset infrastructure such as DLT trading facilities under existing frameworks amended for that purpose.
formal_definition: Established under the Federal Act on the Swiss Financial Market Supervisory Authority (Finanzmarktaufsichtsgesetz, FINMASA/FINMAG) of 22 June 2007, SR 956.1. FINMA is Switzerland's single integrated prudential supervisor for banks, insurers, securities firms/dealers, collective investment schemes, and financial market infrastructure (Art. 3 FINMASA), and is the licensing and supervisory authority for the Art. 1b Banking Act 'fintech licence', the FMIA 'DLT trading facility' category, and CISA-authorised funds referenced elsewhere in this jurisdiction's coverage. FINMA describes its approach to crypto-asset business as applying existing law 'in a consistently technology-neutral way, i.e. in keeping with the "same risks, same rules" principle' (FINMA press release, 29 September 2021).
